bbc.title: Dozens killed as Israeli special forces raid Lebanese village in search of 40-year-old remains
bbc.description: Overnight, one Israeli operation saw at least 41 people killed and 40 injured, according to the Lebanese health ministry.

bbc.title: War fuels debate in Cyprus over UK military bases
bbc.description: “British Bases Out” is the slogan of Cypriot protesters after a drone strike targeted the RAF Akrotiri airbase.

bbc.title: US embassy in Oslo hit by explosion, Norway police say
bbc.description: The embassy sustained minor damage but no injuries were reported after the blast in the Norwegian capital.

bbc.title: Boy, 12, among six dead as tornadoes hit Michigan and Oklahoma
bbc.description: The storms uprooted trees, downed power lines, damaged buildings and ripped the roofs off some houses.

bbc.title: Iranian ambassador warns UK to be 'very careful' about further involvement in war
bbc.description: Seyed Ali Mousavi says Iran has a "right to self-defence" if the UK directly joins US-Israeli attacks.

bbc.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah unseats Nepal's ex-PM as he heads for victory
bbc.description: Thursday's general election was the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September.

bbc.title: Heavy rains and flooding kills at least 23 in Nairobi
bbc.description: Flights in and out of Nairobi Airport have been disrupted while some roads in the capital are submerged.

bbc.title: Swiss to vote on right-wing push to slash licence fee for public broadcaster
bbc.description: The move is backed by the right-wing Swiss People's Party, which says the current fee is unjustified because of the high cost of living.

wsj.title: Palestinians Stream Back to Northern Gaza on Foot
wsj.description: Israel allowed displaced Gazans to begin crossing a military zone that bisects the enclave after a deadlock over hostage releases was broken.

wsj.title: Leading China Property Developer Reports Huge loss, in Sign of Widening Real-Estate Woes
wsj.description: Troubles at Vanke raise questions about the continued spread of the property crisis and whether the Chinese state will step in.

wsj.title: Freed Israeli Hostages Still Had Shrapnel in Their Bodies From Oct. 7 Attack
wsj.description: Some of the women were held alone for extended periods and spent eight months in tunnels, an Israeli medical official said.

wsj.title: Suspected Sabotage of Deep-Sea Cable Triggers First NATO-Led Response
wsj.description: The alliance mounted its first coordinated response to a suspected sabotage campaign against critical infrastructure after another cable was severed in the Baltic Sea.

wsj.title: Rwanda-Backed Rebels Enter Congo's Safe-Haven City
wsj.description: Residents of Goma reported gunfire and shelling after rebels overran Congolese troops. U.N. officials estimated that more than one million displaced people were now inside the city.

wsj.title: Cocaine-Funded Gangs Shake Colombia Years After Peace Pact
wsj.description: Attacks by powerful militias against civilians reflect the state’s inaction eight years after a peace accord removed a powerful rebel group from the field.

wsj.title: Italy Supports Saudi Arabia Joining Fighter-Jet Program, PM Meloni Says
wsj.description: The U.K., Italy and Japan have already partnered on the Global Combat Air Programme, which aims to put a new stealth fighter with supersonic capability in the skies by 2035.

wsj.title: Shippers Wary of Red Sea Routes Despite Houthi Pledge to End Targeting
wsj.description: The world’s top three container operators said they fear instability in Gaza and broader regional tensions mean continued danger.

wsj.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
wsj.description: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

wsj.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
wsj.description: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

wsj.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
wsj.description: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

wsj.title: Financial Services Roundup: Market Talk
wsj.description: Gain insight on Experian, SoFi Technologies and more in the latest Market Talks covering Financial Services.

wsj.title: Arabica Coffee Prices Hit Record on U.S., Colombia Tariff Spat
wsj.description: Arabica coffee prices hit a record level, as traders digested the withdrawal of President Trump’s threats to impose tariffs and economic sanctions on Colombia.

wsj.title: Swiss franc, Japanese yen Rise as DeepSeek News Boosts Safe Havens
wsj.description: The Yen and Swiss Franc were stronger against the dollar as investors sought safe havens after Chinese start-up DeepSeek’s new AI model hit U.S. tech stocks.

wsj.title: Natural Gas Falls on Shifting Weather Forecasts
wsj.description: U.S. natural gas futures fell after weekend updates took some of the chill out of early February weather forecasts.

wsj.title: Building-Products Distributor QXO Launches Hostile Bid for Beacon
wsj.description: QXO is taking its offer directly to shareholders after being rebuffed on several occasions.

bloomberg.title: US Inflation Gauges Likely Diverged Before War in Iran

bloomberg.title: Aramco Shares Surge Most Since 2023 as War Roils Energy Markets
bloomberg.description: Saudi Aramco jumped the most since May 2023 on Sunday as the Iran war entered its second week, prompting supply disruptions that may send oil prices higher when global markets reopen.

bloomberg.title: China Steps Up Investment in Africa's Railway Infrastructure

bloomberg.title: Women in China Run Nearly $1 Trillion of Funds, Cailian Reports
bloomberg.description: Female fund managers in China oversaw a total of 6.8 trillion yuan ($984 billion) in assets as of March 6, Cailian reported on Sunday, citing data from Wind Information Co.

bloomberg.title: UAE and Kuwait Start Oil Output Cuts After Hormuz Blockage
bloomberg.description: The United Arab Emirates and Kuwait started reducing oil production, as the near-closure of the crucial Strait of Hormuz ripples through energy markets and affects global supply.

bloomberg.title: Traders Snatch Up Derivatives as Risks Grow: Credit Weekly
bloomberg.description: War in Iran. A weakening US jobs market. Artificial intelligence and the potential demise of whole industries. Growing pressure on private credit.

bloomberg.title: US Offers $20 Billion Reinsurance Plan to Spur Gulf Oil Flow
bloomberg.description: The Trump administration announced a $20 billion reinsurance program aimed at reviving shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, where traffic has all but stopped amid the US and Israeli attacks on Iran.

bloomberg.description: A proposal to finance Poland’s defense expenditure by using profits from its gold reserves needs to be clearly communicated to markets, a central bank policymaker said.

bloomberg.description: Taiwan’s premier made a personal trip to Japan and appeared in public to watch the island’s baseball team in action, a rare visit that risks deteriorating Tokyo’s ties with China further.
